level_signals: Extensive experience leading localization programs required
Expected to provide strategic framework and technical guidance cross-functionally

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive experience leading localization programs for high-growth consumer products.
Strong project management skills with demonstrated experience using Jira and localization tools like Lokalise.
Proven experience in managing professional service delivery from translators and translation agencies.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple stakeholders and optimize complex localization processes.
Deep understanding of cultural adaptation and internationalization (i18n) best practices.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field; fluency in English is mandatory, while additional language proficiency is a significant plus.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: additional language proficiency is a significant plus.

scope.surface: UX localization across 16 countries and six distinct brands within HelloTech
primary_metrics_inferred: Delivery speed and SLA adherence with vendors
AI automation efficiency (machine translation, LLM-assisted QA)
core_responsibilities: Refine UX Localization strategy in collaboration with Head of Content Design & Localization
Lead end-to-end service delivery for UX content localization
Build AI-enabled localization workflows including machine translation integration and LLM-assisted reviews
Provide technical guidance to product managers, engineers, and designers
Act as primary point of contact for external translation vendors
Conduct regular audits of localization workflows within Lokalise and Jira
Establish rigorous testing systems for live environments
Share localization best practices across HelloTech

green_flags: Clear primary stakeholder named (Head of Content Design & Localization)
Specific tools mentioned (Lokalise, Jira) indicate genuine technical context
Explicit scope: 16 countries, 6 brands — concrete scale signal
Modern AI/LLM-enabled workflow focus indicates forward-looking team
JD explicitly welcomes candidates who don't tick every box
Warsaw office described with concrete amenities
red_flags: Role is framed as 'service provider' and 'external expert' — may indicate contractor/freelance engagement rather than full-time employment
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Seniority level not quantified (no years of experience stated explicitly)
Location type and remote eligibility not explicitly clarified beyond Warsaw office mention

location.type (inferred as onsite from 'Work with HelloFresh in Warsaw' and office access described as a benefit)

ambiguities: The JD repeatedly refers to the role holder as 'service provider' and 'external expert' which may indicate this is a contractor/freelance engagement rather than a permanent employee role. This is not fully clarified anywhere in the JD.

Location type is not explicitly stated as onsite vs hybrid; 'Access to workspace at Warsaw Centre Point' is listed as a benefit, which may imply hybrid is possible. Treated as onsite given Warsaw is the only city mentioned and no remote option is stated.
